pr.title: Name some known fighter struct members
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Renamed several known Fighter struct callback/bitfield members from offset-style placeholders to semantic names and propagated those names across fighter, draw, lib, walljump, common-action, and character special-move code. The PR was behavior-preserving for GALE01 according to the decomp.dev report, with no human review discussion in the slice.
what_changed: In Fighter, renamed callback field fp+21F4 from x21F4 to hurtbox_detect_cb and updated uses such as Captain Falcon Side-B setup assigning ftCa_SpecialS_OnDetect.
Renamed bitfield fp+2218:3 from x2218_b3 to reflecting and updated reflector-related code in guard, Fox reflector, Mario cape, Mewtwo Confusion, Zelda Nayru's Love, Ness bat, and Yoshi guard paths.

Renamed bitfield fp+221E:0 from x221E_b0 to invisible and updated invisibility/visibility state handling across barrel, Kirby/Yoshi egg capture, Master Hand capture/throw, Mewtwo/Zelda/Sheik teleport-like specials, material sleep, ftlib, and draw code.
Renamed bitfield fp+2222:1 from x2222_b1 to can_multijump and updated initialization/checks for Kirby/Jigglypuff-style aerial jump behavior.
Renamed bitfield fp+2224:7 from x2224_b7 to can_walljump and updated character on-load setup for walljump-capable fighters plus ftwalljump checks.
Renamed bitfield fp+2228:6 from x2228_b6 to used_tether and updated aerial tether-grab gating/reset logic.
Added explanatory comments in types.h for x2221_b4/x2221_b5/x2221_b6 as parasol-related and x2222_b0 as a DK cargo-grab capability flag, without fully renaming those fields.
Cleaned several boolean expressions while touching sites, replacing comparisons like field != false, integer 0/1 assignments, and bitwise-looking tests with true/false and direct boolean checks.
Simplified Link SpecialN switch fallthrough by moving the common return true after the switch, removing an explicit fall-through comment while preserving behavior.
smart_moves: Centralized semantic knowledge in src/melee/ft/types.h first, then mechanically propagated member names to all users so future callsites expose behavior directly instead of raw offsets.

Used conservative names for fields with clear cross-file evidence: can_walljump is set in on-load for known walljump characters and checked in ftWallJump_8008169C; used_tether is tested/set in tether aerial catch and reset on motion changes; invisible gates draw/lib visibility and capture/teleport states.
Kept less-certain fields as placeholder names with comments rather than over-renaming, e.g. parasol-related x2221_b4/x2221_b5/x2221_b6 and DK cargo-grab x2222_b0.
Converted touched flags to C boolean style, which makes bitfield intent clearer and reduces noisy comparisons in future decomp work.
Relied on the decomp.dev report showing no GALE01 changes, supporting that the broad rename/refactor remained matching/behavior-preserving.
decomp_lessons: When a Fighter bitfield is used across many character modules, a well-evidenced semantic rename can significantly improve readability without requiring function matching changes.
Name fields according to the behavior shown by both initialization and consumers: on-load capability flags, per-state runtime flags, and callback slots each provide different evidence.
For uncertain struct members, prefer comments documenting observed associations over premature semantic names.
Propagate struct member renames across common code and character-specific code in one PR to prevent mixed offset-style and semantic names from coexisting.
Boolean cleanup is safest when adjacent to confirmed bool bitfields and when decomp.dev confirms no codegen changes.
naming_conventions: Fighter callback fields should use callback-suffixed semantic names when purpose is known, e.g. hurtbox_detect_cb.
Capability flags use can_* naming, e.g. can_walljump and can_multijump.
Runtime state flags use descriptive present/past-participle names, e.g. reflecting, invisible, used_tether.
Unknown bitfields can retain x####_b# names with Doxygen-style inline comments when evidence is partial, e.g. ///< parasol-related and ///< can cargo grab? only set for DK.
Use true/false for boolean bitfields rather than 1/0 where matching permits.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Performed source-level semantic renames and boolean-expression cleanups while preserving generated code; decomp.dev reported 'No changes' for GALE01.
Direct boolean checks such as if (fp->reflecting), !fighter->invisible, and if (!(flags & Ft_MF_SkipParasol)) were accepted in touched contexts.
Replacing x2218_b3/x221E_b0/x2224_b7 style accesses with named bitfields did not alter layout because offsets and bit positions in Fighter remained unchanged.
The Link SpecialN switch was refactored from default/fall-through return true to a post-switch return true without reported binary changes.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported for GALE01 from 53a37e9 to 4614eec: 'No changes'.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining x2218_b4 users to determine the second Mewtwo Confusion reflect-related flag semantics.
Search x2221_b4 x2221_b5 x2221_b6 parasol-related uses to refine names beyond comments.
Search x2222_b0 uses and DK on-load/init code to validate a can_cargo_grab or similar name.
Audit remaining Fighter x221E bitfields adjacent to invisible for draw/collision/item visibility semantics.
Search remaining x21F8 or nearby Fighter callback fields for similar callback-slot naming opportunities.
